meta.title: Sofra — Restaurant software with a warm heart
meta.description: QR menus, live orders, reservations and loyalty — one kitchen-friendly platform, proven in a real restaurant every single service. Sofra means a table laid for guests.

hero.subtitle: Sofra is the warm-hearted restaurant platform: QR menus, live orders, reservations and loyalty — everything your front-of-house and kitchen need, on one table.
hero.note: A «sofra» is a table laid for guests. That's the whole philosophy.
hero.proof: Running every service at RUMI Restaurant, Geneva — orders, kitchen, reservations and printers, live in production.

features.label: What's cooking
features.title: Everything on one table
features.subtitle: Six courses, no filler. Each one battle-tested in a real restaurant before it ever reached this page.

qr.title: QR menus & ordering
qr.text: Guests scan, browse and order from the table — with photos, allergens and customizations. No app to install.

live.title: Live order boards
live.text: Kitchen, cashier and servers each get their own live view. New orders arrive the second they're placed.

reservations.title: Reservations
reservations.text: Table planning, working hours and availability — guests book online, you approve with a tap.

loyalty.title: Loyalty points
loyalty.text: Fidelity points that bring regulars back, with discount rules you control per customer group.

printing.title: Kitchen tickets, printed
printing.text: A companion app drives your thermal printers — orders hit the kitchen on paper, exactly where you want them.

languages.title: 10 languages built in
languages.text: Your menu speaks your guests' languages out of the box — from French to Dutch to Arabic.

one.title: Tell us about your place
one.text: Name, menu, tables, hours. A conversation, not a setup wizard.

two.title: We lay your sofra
two.text: We set up your menu, QR codes, printers and staff views — and hand you the keys.

three.title: You serve
three.text: Orders flow, tickets print, regulars collect points. We keep the plates spinning behind the scenes.

footer.tagline: A sofra is a table laid for guests.
footer.madeIn: Cooked up in the Netherlands, first served in Geneva

what.a: Sofra is a restaurant management platform: QR menus and online ordering, live order boards for kitchen, cashier and servers, reservations, loyalty points and thermal-printer tickets — all in one place. It grew out of the system running every service at RUMI Restaurant in Geneva.

pricing.a: During early access, founding restaurants pay nothing — setup and support are on the house, and founding restaurants keep a lifetime discount when regular pricing arrives. Regular plans will be simple flat monthly pricing with optional add-on modules.

payments.q: Can my guests pay online — including TWINT?
payments.a: Online payments are on the roadmap as an add-on module, with local payment methods like TWINT in Switzerland and iDEAL in the Netherlands planned alongside cards. Today, orders flow to your counter and you charge as you always have.

languages.q: Which languages does the menu support?
languages.a: Ten languages out of the box — English, French, German, Dutch, Italian, Spanish, Turkish, Arabic, Russian and Chinese. Your guests order in their own language from the same menu.

printing.q: Does it work with my kitchen printer?
printing.a: Yes. A companion app (Windows and Android) drives ESC/POS thermal printers over the network, so orders print in the kitchen exactly where you want them — no extra hardware subscription.

who.q: Who is behind Sofra, and is it actually used anywhere?
who.a: Sofra is built by a small team based in the Netherlands, and it runs every live service at RUMI Restaurant in Geneva (rumirestaurant.ch) — orders, kitchen board, reservations and printers have been in production there since June 2026. We onboard new restaurants personally.

domain.q: Can I use my own domain name?
domain.a: Yes. Every restaurant gets a free sofra subdomain, and you can connect a domain you already own — or we register a brand-new one for you during setup.

languagesSection.body: Geneva is an international city, and RUMI's menu meets it: guests browse and order in English, French, German, Dutch, Italian, Spanish, Turkish, Arabic, Russian or Chinese — same menu, same kitchen.

numbers.body: We publish only figures we can stand behind. Detailed service statistics from RUMI will appear here once we can share them properly — until then, open the live menu and judge the product the honest way.

meta.title: Sofra vs GloriaFood (2026): an honest comparison
meta.description: GloriaFood is a free online-ordering system with paid add-on modules; Sofra is a full-service restaurant platform from the Netherlands. A fair, dated comparison with sources.

compare.label: Side by side
compare.title: Sofra vs GloriaFood
compare.subtitle: Two honest ways to put a restaurant online — compared fairly, with sources and a date.
compare.intro1: GloriaFood — part of Oracle — is one of the best-known online-ordering systems: the core product is free, and you add paid modules as you grow. Sofra is a younger, full-service platform built in the Netherlands and proven in daily service in Geneva: ordering, reservations, loyalty and kitchen printing as one system, set up for you personally.
compare.intro2: Both are honest choices. Which one fits depends on how much you want to do yourself — the table below sticks to facts we verified on GloriaFood's own pages.

table.caption: Sofra and GloriaFood, feature by feature

pricing.label: Pricing model
pricing.sofra: Early access: on the house for founding restaurants, with a lifetime discount when regular pricing arrives. After that: a simple flat monthly price with optional modules.
pricing.gf: Free core: no monthly fee, no commission per order. Paid modules: online payments US$29/month, advanced promotions US$19/month, POS US$49/month per location (two-year commitment).

ordering.label: QR & online ordering
ordering.sofra: Included — QR menus at the table and online ordering, with photos, allergens and customizations. No app to install.
ordering.gf: Included free — ordering widget for your website and Facebook page, plus QR dine-in ordering.

reservations.label: Table reservations
reservations.sofra: Included — online booking, table planning, working hours, one-tap approval.
reservations.gf: Reservation widget included; taking booking deposits costs US$0.50 per guest on accepted bookings.

loyalty.label: Loyalty & promotions
loyalty.sofra: Included — fidelity points plus discount rules per customer group.
loyalty.gf: Promotions and coupons in the free plan; the advanced promo-marketing module costs US$19/month.

printing.label: Kitchen printing
printing.sofra: A companion app (Windows and Android) drives ESC/POS network thermal printers — tickets per station, no extra subscription.
printing.gf: Their free order-taking app (Android and iOS) connects to a thermal printer and prints accepted orders automatically.

languages.label: Languages
languages.sofra: Guests browse and order in 10 languages — on the same menu.
languages.gf: Product interface available in a dozen languages, translated by their community — see their translation page for specifics.

hosting.label: Hosting & domains
hosting.sofra: Runs on Sofra-managed infrastructure in the EU; every restaurant gets its own domain — yours, or one we register for you.
hosting.gf: Cloud service operated by Oracle (GloriaFood is an Oracle product).

setup.label: Setup & support
setup.sofra: White-glove onboarding — we set up your menu, QR codes, printers and staff views with you, and stay a direct line away.
setup.gf: Self-service — you set the system up yourself through their admin panel and documentation.

payments.label: Online payments
payments.sofra: On the roadmap as an add-on module — local methods like TWINT and iDEAL planned alongside cards. Today you charge at the counter as usual.
payments.gf: Available as a paid module — online / card payments at US$29/month.

compare.disclaimer: GloriaFood facts above were checked on their public pages in July 2026 and can change — always confirm on their pricing page. GloriaFood is an Oracle product; all names and trademarks belong to their owners. Prices shown are theirs, in US dollars, excluding taxes.

free.a: The core is. Their pricing page states no commission per order and no monthly fee for the basic ordering system; online payments (US$29/month), advanced promotions (US$19/month), the POS (US$49/month per location, two-year commitment), a sales-optimized website and branded mobile apps are paid extras. For many small restaurants the free tier is genuinely enough.

cost.a: During early access, founding restaurants pay nothing — setup and support are on the house, and they keep a lifetime discount when regular pricing arrives. Regular plans will be simple flat monthly pricing with optional add-on modules.

pick.a: If you mainly want a free ordering widget on a website you already run, and you're happy to set it up yourself, GloriaFood is a solid, proven choice. If you want one system for ordering, reservations, loyalty and kitchen printing — set up with you personally, in your guests' languages, on EU infrastructure — that's exactly what Sofra is built for.

fitGf.title: When GloriaFood is a fine fit

items.widget: You want a free ordering widget on your existing website, today.
items.self: You're comfortable setting up and running the system yourself.
items.freeTier: The free tier already covers what you need — no reason to pay more.

items.one: You want ordering, reservations, loyalty and kitchen printing as one system, not separate modules.
items.languages: Your guests speak more than one language — Sofra serves ten from the same menu.
items.hands: You'd rather have someone lay the table with you — and stay one message away — than do it all alone.
items.domain: You want your restaurant on its own domain, in your own currency, hosted in the EU.

sources.title: Sources
sources.note: Every GloriaFood fact on this page was verified on {date} against their public pages:

compare.ctaBody: The fastest way to compare is to see Sofra running in a real restaurant — or on your own tables.

meta.title: What's new at Sofra — changelog
meta.description: Dated notes on what's new at Sofra: features, languages, billing, security and reliability. Short and honest — if it's listed here, it's live.

changelog.label: Fresh from the kitchen
changelog.title: What's new
changelog.subtitle: Short, dated notes on what just came out of our kitchen. If it's listed here, it's live.

currency.title: Your currency, end to end
currency.body: Every restaurant on Sofra now runs in its own currency — menu, orders, tickets and receipts all speak the same one. We also laid the groundwork for per-restaurant looks, with a classic and a craft template in the making.

hardening.title: Security & reliability week
hardening.body: Uptime monitoring around the clock, error tracking and performance checks on every release, and strict security headers with a tight content-security policy across the whole site.

billing.title: Subscriptions, the boring way
billing.body: Billing is live: restaurants pay by a simple recurring subscription, processed by Mollie. Behind the counter, a new restaurant registry in the admin panel keeps every setup in one place.

languages.title: Six languages on the site, ten at the table
languages.body: sofrapiwas.com now speaks English, French, German, Dutch, Turkish and Arabic — right-to-left included. Guests at Sofra restaurants order in ten languages, and new restaurants are set up automatically, each with its own domain.

launch.title: Sofra opens its doors
launch.body: sofrapiwas.com is live — and so is the partner program: agencies and independents apply, are approved personally, and follow every restaurant from first call to live service, with transparent commission tracking.
